description: Letter in Judaeo-Arabic. Possibly in the hand of the court clerk Yosef b. Shemuel b. Seʿadya. The letter opens with condolences for the addressee's loss. Then the sender goes into his own problems: his son Abū Manṣūr does not pay attention to what he says nor accept advice from him. The son has married, or plans to marry, a bad woman. (Information from Mediterranean Society, V, p. 122.) On verso there are accounts in Arabic script and Greek/Coptic numerals. OZ. ASE.
tags: personal, family, complaint, condolence, bad son
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית. פותח בתנחומים לנמען, ולאחר מכן עובר לבעיות השולח: בנו אבו מנצור אינו שומע בקולו ולא מקבל עצותיו, ונשא או מתכנן לשאת אשה רעה.
